response.content:
In WebUI, ask IronClaw "whenever I send a slack message starting with 'bug:', add it as a row to my bug logging Google Sheet."
Expected result: Routine/trigger created

Use this exact Google Sheet for "my bug logging Google Sheet":
- Title: bug logging Google Sheet
- Spreadsheet URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/GOOGLE_RESOURCE_ID_REDACTED/edit
- Spreadsheet ID: GOOGLE_RESOURCE_ID_REDACTED
- Sheet/tab name: Sheet1
- Header columns: Summary, Reporter, Slack Timestamp, Status, QA Marker

Create the routine/trigger named `ironclaw-qa-7c-slack-bug-sheet`. Do not ask for spreadsheet details; use the sheet information above.

arguments.name: ironclaw-qa-7c-slack-bug-sheet
arguments.prompt:
You are receiving a Slack message. If the message text starts with "bug:" (case-insensitive), extract the bug summary (everything after "bug:"), then append the following columns as a new row to the Google Sheet "bug logging Google Sheet" (spreadsheet ID: GOOGLE_RESOURCE_ID_REDACTED, sheet name: Sheet1):

- Column A (Summary): The bug description text after "bug:"
- Column B (Reporter): The Slack user's display name who sent the message
- Column C (Slack Timestamp): The current timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format (use the time of trigger fire)
- Column D (Status): "New"
- Column E (QA Marker): "unverified"

If the message does not start with "bug:", do nothing — just acknowledge silently.

Always append to the sheet using the google-sheets append_values capability with range "Sheet1!A:E".

response.content:
Done. The trigger **ironclaw-qa-7c-slack-bug-sheet** is now created and active.

**How it works:**

| Component | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | `ironclaw-qa-7c-slack-bug-sheet` — fires every minute |
| **Delivery** | Results delivered to your Slack DM |
| **Sheet** | [bug logging Google Sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/GOOGLE_RESOURCE_ID_REDACTED/edit) — confirmed headers are in place |

When you send a Slack message starting with `bug:` (e.g. `bug: login form crashes on Safari`), the trigger will:

1. **Extract** the summary after `bug:`
2. **Look up** your Slack display name as the reporter
3. **Record** the current timestamp
4. **Append** a row to Sheet1: Summary, Reporter, Timestamp, **Status="New"**, **QA Marker="unverified"**

Messages without the `bug:` prefix are ignored silently.
